label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how the students performed in the experiment based on the article. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall what the study found. The article mentions that students were divided into two groups: one using first-person and the other second-person when writing advice to themselves. The results said that those using the second-person completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Also, when it came to exercising, the second-person group reported being more positive and planned to exercise more. So looking at the options: A) First-person had better attitude. But the article says the opposite, that second-person had better attitude. So A is wrong. B) Those who talked to themselves completed most puzzles. Wait, the study had them writing down advice, not necessarily talking out loud. The article even mentions that the researchers asked them to write, not say it. So B might not be correct because it mentions talking, while the study used writing. Plus, the correct group was second-person writers, not necessarily those
